title: notifications core — HTTP middleware
description: Two rate limiters on top of foundation's canonical middleware pipeline. Placed AFTER `throttle:foundation-base` (which handles baseline 60/min authed) so per-surface limits stack additively — a user can hit their base budget AND the mark-seen budget in the same minute without either bleeding into the other.

middleware.description: Bursty by design — users open a tabbed inbox + mark 20+ notifications seen in seconds. Higher-than-base budget prevents accidental UX blocking; per-user keyed.

returnsOn429.hint: Batching client-side into `POST /mark-all-seen` avoids this limit — use that endpoint for large inbox drops.

middleware.description: Preference updates are settings-page changes — rare per user but expensive on the DB (bulk upsert + cache invalidation + audit rows). Tight budget catches accidental PATCH loops from a buggy toggle-grid client.

returnsOn429.hint: Debounce toggle changes client-side or send them as a batch PATCH.

notes: Both limiters register in foundation's `RateLimiter::for()` bindings via NotificationsServiceProvider.

Channel modules ship their OWN limiters for provider-facing routes (mail provider webhooks, sms STOP handlers, push register). Core's limiters cover only the tenant-facing inbox + preferences surface.
The `signed` middleware on the unsubscribe route is Laravel's built-in — not declared here.
